---
title: "Title of the Paper"
author: 
- "Author Name1"
- "Author Name2"
date: last-modified
date-format: "MMMM YYYY"
abstract: |
  This is the abstract of the paper. It will be formatted automatically according to the style file defined in `paper.sty`, with no indentation and specific spacing.
format: 
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    pdf-engine: pdflatex
    # Load the adapted style file
    include-in-header: 
      - text: |
          \usepackage{paper}
    # Map geometry settings from the original style
    geometry:
      - top=1in
      - bottom=1.2in
      - left=1.3in
      - right=1.3in
      - footskip=0.7in
    # Map hyperref settings from the original style
    hyperrefoptions:
      - hidelinks
      - hypertexnames=false
      - pdfpagemode=UseNone
      - pdfdisplaydoctitle=true
    # Ensure natbib is used for citations
    cite-method: natbib
bibliography: references.bib
---

# Introduction

This is a Quarto template using the custom `paper.sty`. The fonts used are Source Serif Pro for text and Source Code Pro for code.

## Math

The math fonts are set to use `MnSymbol` and `mathastext`.

$$
f(x) = \int_{0}^{\infty} e^{-x^2} dx
$$

## Theorems

The theorem environments are defined in the style file.

\begin{theorem}
This is a theorem styled with small caps heading and italic body.
\end{theorem}

\begin{proof}
This is the proof environment.
\end{proof}

# Results

You can use standard Quarto markdown for tables and figures, and the style file will center them and format captions automatically.

| Column 1 | Column 2 |
|----------|----------|
| A        | 10       |
| B        | 20       |

: Sample Table

# Conclusion

The appendix logic is handled automatically after the bibliography.

\newpage
